title: Synotis glomerata
intro: Synotis glomerata, also known as the Himalayan Synotis, is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Synotis glomerata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Synotis glomerata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to India and has been classified as a member of the genus Synotis, which is part of the tribe Lactuceae. Within the Asteraceae family, Synotis glomerata is further grouped under the subfamily Cichorioideae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. Further research is necessary to confirm the accuracy of this classification.
